corpus.name: channels
corpus.label: Two-channel record
corpus.exhibit_label: Formal channel checked against the casual channel
corpus.exhibit_note: Layer 6 compares a sender's formal claim against the same sender's own words elsewhere.

messages.body: Sending you this week's summary as agreed.
For clarity, I have always kept you fully informed about every appointment with the dentist.
The appointment calendar has been shared with you well ahead of time.
My lawyer says the current arrangement should remain in place.

findings.summary: Elevated - a claim contradicted by the sender's own words in another channel + independent domains converging on one anchor independently agree.

signals.detail: Quinn claims 'kept the other parent informed' here; other channel shows 'withheld a disclosure' (seq 2)

messages.body: For clarity, I have always kept you fully informed about every appointment with the dentist.

narration.plain: Elevated finding at seqs [2] (L4, L6). Elevated - a claim contradicted by the sender's own words in another channel + independent domains converging on one anchor independently agree.
narration.blanc: Here the threads meet: seqs [2], layers L4, L6. Not a hunch, but corroboration.

findings.summary: Low - a decision deferred to an outside authority (single layer; not corroborated).

messages.body: My lawyer says the current arrangement should remain in place.

narration.plain: Low finding at seqs [4] (L1). Low - a decision deferred to an outside authority (single layer; not corroborated).
narration.blanc: Seqs [4] remain low. Suggestive, perhaps, but the method refuses to elevate a lone thread.

narration.plain:
Convergence report - 5 messages; 1 elevated finding(s), 1 low.

ELEVATED - seqs [2] (layers L4, L6)
  Elevated - a claim contradicted by the sender's own words in another channel + independent domains converging on one anchor independently agree.
    - [L6] Quinn claims 'kept the other parent informed' here; other channel shows 'withheld a disclosure' (seq 2)
    - [L4] appointment across medical, schedule

low - seqs [4] (L1): a substantive signal with nothing to corroborate it; not corroborated.

A finding is elevated only when independent layers agree; lone signals stay low by design.
narration.blanc:
Gather round, if you please. Let me walk you through it, from the beginning.

We have before us 5 messages. Most are precisely what they appear to be. But not all. Permit me to draw the threads together, one at a time.

Begin here. Turn your attention to seqs [2].
   Here, one face for the record, another off it. seq 2: "For clarity, I have always kept you fully informed about every appointment with the dentist."
   And corroborating, quietly: the same matter, pressing in from another domain.
   2 independent threads (L4, L6), arriving by separate roads at the selfsame doorstep, having never once conferred. Elevated - a claim contradicted by the sender's own words in another channel + independent domains converging on one anchor independently agree. That, I do not call coincidence.

Now - you will want to point me to the loose ends. I have not forgotten them.
   Seqs [4] (L1) - a lone voice - seq 4: "My *lawyer says* the current arrangement should remain in place.". Suggestive, perhaps. But it stands alone, uncorroborated, and I'll not hang a case on a hunch. A red herring. Set aside.

So. The shape of it is unmistakable. Where the threads converge, I have shown you - the donut, and the hole at its heart. I map the hole; I do not fill it. The truth of intent, that I leave to the court.

composition_narration.plain:
Patterns (recurring structure above individual findings):
  - [named chain] two-faced @ seqs [2] - cross_channel_divergence => two-faced
composition_narration.blanc:
Now - a pattern is a habit, and habits do tell tales:
  - two-faced, a named chain, at seqs [2]. cross_channel_divergence => two-faced.
